steps.title: Identify roots from the graph
steps.content: Reading the graph, the x-intercepts occur at $x = -5$, $x = 0$, and $x = 4$. This immediately eliminates choices C and D, which have roots at $x = -4$ and $x = 5$ instead.

steps.title: Check end behavior to determine degree
steps.content:
Observe the behavior at the far left and far right of the graph:
  • As $x \to -\infty$, $y \to -\infty$ (left end points downward)
  • As $x \to +\infty$, $y \to -\infty$ (right end points downward)
Both ends negative indicates an even-degree polynomial with negative leading coefficient. Choice A, $y=-\frac{1}{10}x(x-4)(x+5)$, is a cubic (degree 3, odd) so it fails this test—a cubic has opposite end behaviors. Choice B, $y=-\frac{1}{10}x(x-4)(x+5)^{2}$, is a quartic (degree 4, even), which matches.

steps.title: Confirm root multiplicity at x = −5
steps.content: At $x = -5$, the graph touches the x-axis without crossing—it bounces back down. This behavior indicates even multiplicity. In choice B, the factor $(x+5)^2$ gives $x = -5$ multiplicity 2, which produces exactly this touching behavior. That matches choice B — B is correct.
